page: Amazon Web Services m8g.16xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Built on the 64-bit ARM architecture (arm64), this instance features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, with a single thread per core. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache, alongside hardware-accelerated AES cryptography. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8g.16xlarge demonstrates exceptional memory performance and multi-threaded efficiency. It achieves top-tier status in memory bandwidth tests, reaching 7394.64 GB/sec for cached reads and up to 464.59 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Multi-core processing capabilities are highly competitive, as evidenced by top-tier results in Geekbench multi-core scores, software compilation with Clang, and Passmark database operations. The instance also excels in high-throughput network and database tasks, delivering 131.7 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 124.08 GB/sec in static web serving throughput.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64-based Graviton architecture offers high resource density and excellent performance-per-resource characteristics compared to traditional architectures, particularly for memory-bound and highly parallelized workloads. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, and the absence of discrete GPU hardware limits its utility for heavy graphics or massive parallel AI training. This instance is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation pipelines, high-throughput web serving, Redis caching layers, and medium-scale database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services m8g.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a 64-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. In benchmarks, it delivers top-tier memory bandwidth, reaching 464.59 GB/sec for uncached reads, and excels in multi-threaded tasks, software compilation, and database operations, as shown by its 131.7 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. The m8g.16xlarge offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for ARM-native workloads, making it highly suitable for high-throughput web serving, caching layers, software development pipelines, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.16xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 instance featuring 64 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 256 GB RAM, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.16xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 instance with 64 Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth.
tagline: A 64-core ARM64 general-purpose instance delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and high-throughput database performance for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 64-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 processor at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
Delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and 131.7 million Redis SET operations per second
Provides a 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for high-throughput web serving, caching, and software compilation
